title: Troubleshooting GlobalProtect Connection Issues - Variation 0
content: GlobalProtect connection failures often occur due to incorrect portal or gateway settings. Check the logs for 'Authentication failed' or 'Portal unreachable'. Ensure the client certificate is valid and not expired. If using SAML, verify the identity provider configuration. For HIP check failures, ensure the endpoint is compliant with the policy.

title: Panorama Commit Validation Error: Reference Dependency - Variation 0
content: A common Panorama commit error is 'cannot delete object still referenced'. This happens when you try to remove an IPsec tunnel or IKE gateway that is still used in a template or device group. To fix this, identify the reference path in the error message and remove the object from the referencing configuration before committing.

title: Prisma Access Remote Network Onboarding - Variation 0
content: When onboarding a remote network in Prisma Access, ensure the IPsec tunnel configuration matches the service connection requirements. Common issues include IKE phase 1 or phase 2 mismatch. Use 'show vpn ike-sa' and 'show vpn ipsec-sa' to debug. Verify that the routing is correctly configured in the cloud services portal.

title: GlobalProtect HIP Enforcement and Compliance - Variation 0
content: HIP enforcement prevents non-compliant devices from accessing the network. If a user is blocked, check the HIP match logs on the gateway. Common reasons include missing antivirus, disabled firewall, or outdated patches. Ensure the GlobalProtect agent is reporting the host information correctly to the portal.

title: IPsec VPN Tunnel Down Troubleshooting - Variation 0
content: If an IPsec VPN tunnel is down, first check the IKE phase 1 status. Look for 'IKE protocol notification' in the system logs. Verify the pre-shared key, peer IP, and proposal settings. For phase 2, ensure the proxy IDs or traffic selectors match on both ends of the tunnel.

title: Prisma Access Remote Network Onboarding - Variation 54
content: When onboarding a remote network in Prisma Access, ensure the IPsec tunnel configuration matches the service connection requirements. Common issues include IKE phase 1 or phase 2 mismatch. title: GlobalProtect HIP Enforcement and Compliance - Variation 54
content: HIP enforcement prevents non-compliant devices from accessing the network. If a user is blocked, check the HIP match logs on the gateway. content: If an IPsec VPN tunnel is down, first check the IKE phase 1 status. Look for 'IKE protocol notification' in the system logs. Verify the pre-shared key, peer IP, and proposal settings. For phase 2, ensure the proxy IDs or traffic selectors match on both ends of the tunnel.

Use 'show vpn ike-sa' and 'show vpn ipsec-sa' to debug. Verify that the routing is correctly configured in the cloud services portal.

title: GlobalProtect HIP Enforcement and Compliance - Variation 90
content: HIP enforcement prevents non-compliant devices from accessing the network. If a user is blocked, check the HIP match logs on the gateway. Common reasons include missing antivirus, disabled firewall, or outdated patches. Ensure the GlobalProtect agent is reporting the host information correctly to the portal.

title: IPsec VPN Tunnel Down Troubleshooting - Variation 90
content: If an IPsec VPN tunnel is down, first check the IKE phase 1 status. Look for 'IKE protocol notification' in the system logs. Verify the pre-shared key, peer IP, and proposal settings. For phase 2, ensure the proxy IDs or traffic selectors match on both ends of the tunnel.

title: Troubleshooting GlobalProtect Connection Issues - Variation 91
content: GlobalProtect connection failures often occur due to incorrect portal or gateway settings. Check the logs for 'Authentication failed' or 'Portal unreachable'. Ensure the client certificate is valid and not expired. If using SAML, verify the identity provider configuration. For HIP check failures, ensure the endpoint is compliant with the policy.

title: Panorama Commit Validation Error: Reference Dependency - Variation 91
content: A common Panorama commit error is 'cannot delete object still referenced'. This happens when you try to remove an IPsec tunnel or IKE gateway that is still used in a template or device group. To fix this, identify the reference path in the error message and remove the object from the referencing configuration before committing.

Common reasons include missing antivirus, disabled firewall, or outdated patches. Ensure the GlobalProtect agent is reporting the host information correctly to the portal.

title: IPsec VPN Tunnel Down Troubleshooting - Variation 144
content: If an IPsec VPN tunnel is down, first check the IKE phase 1 status. Look for 'IKE protocol notification' in the system logs. Verify the pre-shared key, peer IP, and proposal settings. For phase 2, ensure the proxy IDs or traffic selectors match on both ends of the tunnel.

title: Troubleshooting GlobalProtect Connection Issues - Variation 145
content: GlobalProtect connection failures often occur due to incorrect portal or gateway settings. Check the logs for 'Authentication failed' or 'Portal unreachable'. Ensure the client certificate is valid and not expired. If using SAML, verify the identity provider configuration. For HIP check failures, ensure the endpoint is compliant with the policy.

title: Panorama Commit Validation Error: Reference Dependency - Variation 145
content: A common Panorama commit error is 'cannot delete object still referenced'. This happens when you try to remove an IPsec tunnel or IKE gateway that is still used in a template or device group. To fix this, identify the reference path in the error message and remove the object from the referencing configuration before committing.

title: Prisma Access Remote Network Onboarding - Variation 145
content: When onboarding a remote network in Prisma Access, ensure the IPsec tunnel configuration matches the service connection requirements. Common issues include IKE phase 1 or phase 2 mismatch. Use 'show vpn ike-sa' and 'show vpn ipsec-sa' to debug. Verify that the routing is correctly configured in the cloud services portal.
